item.title: Safety of Janus kinase inhibitors compared to biologic therapy in patients with inflammatory bowel disease.
item.description: Intestinal research - There is a paucity of data regarding the comparative safety of Janus kinase inhibitors (JAKi) compared to biologic therapy in inflammatory bowel disease (IBD). A retrospective cohort study was cond...

item.title: Multivariable genome-wide analysis elucidates the shared genetic architecture, immunosenescence features, and gut-origin therapeutic targets of ulcerative colitis-associated multisystem inflammation.
item.description: Inflammation research : official journal of the European Histamine Research Society ... [et al.] - Over 25% of patients with ulcerative colitis (UC) develop extraintestinal manifestations (EIMs), resulting in significant systemic morbidity. We define the shared genetic foundation of these manife...

item.title: Advanced combination therapy: Janus kinase inhibitors and TNF inhibitors in the treatment of patients with inflammatory bowel disease.

item.title: Hepatitis B virus infection or reactivation and HBV-related liver dysfunction in patients with inflammatory bowel disease receiving infliximab: a nationwide real-world study.
item.description: MedScience - Infliximab (IFX) for inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) treatment may increase the risk of hepatitis B virus (HBV) reactivation, particularly in areas with high HBV prevalence such as China. This stu...

item.title: Effectiveness and safety of mirikizumab in ulcerative colitis: real-world data from the Latium Net.
item.description: BMJ open gastroenterology - Multicenter study - To evaluate the effectiveness and safety of mirikizumab in patients with ulcerative colitis in clinical practice. We conducted a retrospective, multicenter cohort study across five Italian inflamma...

item.title: Improvement in Health-Related Quality of Life in Patients With Moderate to Severe Crohn's Disease Treated With Risankizumab Versus Ustekinumab in the Phase 3B SEQUENCE Study.
item.description: The American journal of gastroenterology - Normalized quality of life represents a crucial long-term treatment goal in Crohn's disease (CD). We evaluated the effect of risankizumab (RZB) versus ustekinumab (UST) on achieving clinically mean...

item.title: Multi-modal therapeutic approaches to inflammatory bowel disease: plant-derived compounds, nanoparticle drug delivery systems, and gene-based interventions.
item.description: Molecular biology reports - Review - Inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) is a chronic, relapsing inflammatory disorder primarily affecting the gastrointestinal tract. The pathogenesis arises from complex interactions among genetic predis...

item.title: Five-year efficacy and safety of guselkumab for moderately to severely active Crohn's disease: Results from the phase 2 GALAXI 1 trial.
item.description: Inflammatory bowel diseases - The randomized, phase 2 GALAXI 1 study evaluated efficacy and safety of guselkumab, a dual-acting IL-23p19 subunit inhibitor, in participants with moderately to severely active Crohn's disease. Eff...

item.title: Efficacy and Safety of Guselkumab for Ulcerative Colitis Through Week 92 of the QUASAR Long-Term Extension Study.
item.description: The American journal of gastroenterology - Evaluate guselkumab efficacy and safety through week 92 of the ongoing QUASAR long-term extension (LTE). Intravenous guselkumab induction responders were randomized (1:1:1) at QUASAR maintenance st...

item.title: Silk fibroin and oxidized glycyrrhizic acid hydrogel loaded with mesalazine nanoparticles: A promising strategy for ulcerative colitis treatment.
item.description: Biomaterials advances - Ulcerative colitis (UC) is a chronic inflammatory bowel disease with rising global incidence, imposing a significant burden on healthcare systems. Current treatments, including aminosalicylic acid ...

item.title: JOSD2 deubiquitinating enzyme: Structure, function, and potential as a therapeutic target.
item.description: Cellular signalling - Review - Ubiquitination, a central post-translational mechanism, shapes the amplitude and duration of cellular signalling. Josephin domain-containing 2 (JOSD2), a Machado-Joseph disease (MJD) family deubiqu...

item.title: Partial responders to intravenous corticosteroids at day 3 benefit from continued therapy in acute severe ulcerative colitis.
item.description: Scientific reports - In acute severe ulcerative colitis (ASUC), early response patterns to intravenous corticosteroids (IVCS) during the first week may shape subsequent disease course. Characterizing these trajectories...

item.title: Rectally Administered Budesonide-Loaded Phase Transformable Premix Offers Improved Recovery of Intestinal Mucosa in Ulcerative Colitis.

item.title: Treatment outcomes of prolonged induction or re-escalation dosing of upadacitinib in patients with inflammatory bowel disease.
item.description: Crohn's & colitis 360 - Letter - Proposed treatment algorithms favor the use of upadacitinib in medically refractory Crohn's disease (CD) and ulcerative colitis (UC). There has not yet been data published regarding efficacy of pro...

item.title: Impact of stopping thiopurines under anti-TNF therapy in inflammatory bowel disease: A population-based study.
item.description: Clinical gastroenterology and hepatology : the official clinical practice journal of the American Gastroenterological Association - The impact of stopping thiopurines after escalating to treatment with an anti-tumor necrosis factor (TNF) agent in patients with inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) is currently unclear. We aimed to a...

item.title: Letter to the editor regarding 'Correlation between infliximab trough levels and postoperative endoscopic recurrence in patients with Crohn's disease'.

item.title: Neutrophil count and platelet-lymphocyte ratio as simple predictors of ustekinumab response in patients with Crohn's disease: a retrospective multicenter study.
item.description: Frontiers in immunology - Multicenter study - Although ustekinumab (UST) has been recommended by guidelines as a parallel first-line biologic agent for Crohn's disease (CD), its efficacy varies among individuals. This study aimed to identify b...

item.title: Recall urticaria caused by vedolizumab: case report and literature review.
item.description: Frontiers in allergy - Case report - Recall urticaria is a rare hypersensitivity phenomenon characterized by the reappearance of urticarial wheals strictly confined to previously exposed skin sites following systemic re-exposure to a ...
